2606.25491.title: HG-Bench: A Benchmark for Multi-Page Handwritten Answer-Region Grounding in Automated Homework Assessment
2606.25491.paper_summary: Automated homework assessment depends not only on recognizing student answers, but also on accurately locating where each answer and each intermediate reasoning step appears in noisy, multi-page handwritten work. This paper addresses the missing evaluation setting of page-aware, two-level answer-region grounding: given a sequence of homework page images, a model must localize complete answer regions and their ordered step-level subregions. We introduce HG-Bench, a benchmark of 500 human-annotated K-12 homework samples curated from a 1,489,278-image source pool, with question-level and step-level boxes linked by a hierarchical containment constraint. HG-Bench is paired with a page-aware evaluation protocol that separately measures complete-answer localization (FA) and step-level decomposition (FSm), revealing whether models truly ground the spatial structure of student reasoning rather than merely parse visible text. Across frontier closed-source APIs and competitive open-weight VLMs, no zero-shot system exceeds 55.22% on FA or 48.22% on FSm, while a GLM-4.6V 9B reference model fine-tuned on ~10k in-domain examples reaches 74.97/72.26. These results identify step-level handwritten grounding as a concrete capability gap and provide a reproducible benchmark, evaluation protocol, and trained reference point for future work on automated homework assessment.
2606.25491.paper_summary_zh: 自動化作業評估不僅依賴於識別學生的答案，還依賴於準確定位每個答案和每個中間推理步驟在嘈雜的多頁手寫作品中出現的位置。本文針對缺失的評估設定進行探討，即頁面感知的兩級答案區域定位：給定一系列作業頁面圖像，模型必須定位完整的答案區域及其有序的步驟級子區域。我們介紹了HG-Bench，這是一個由500個經人類標註的K-12作業樣本組成的基準，這些樣本來自一個包含1,489,278張圖像的來源池，並且問題級和步驟級的框架由層次包含約束相連。HG-Bench配備了頁面感知的評估協議，分別測量完整答案定位（FA）和步驟級分解（FSm），揭示模型是否真正地定位學生推理的空間結構，而不僅僅是解析可見文本。在前沿的封閉源API和競爭的開放權重VLM中，沒有零樣本系統在FA上超過55.22%或在FSm上超過48.22%，而一個在約10k內部範例上微調的GLM-4.6V 9B參考模型達到74.97/72.26。這些結果確定了步驟級手寫定位作為一個具體的能力差距，並提供了一個可重複的基準、評估協議和訓練參考點，以供未來自動化作業評估的研究使用。
2606.25491.author: Chuangxin Zhao et.al.
2606.25491.authors: Chuangxin Zhao, Boyan Shi, Yanling Wang, Yijian LU, Canran Xiao, Jiali Chen, Jun Xia, Yan Wang, Ji Qi, Juanzi Li
